overview: Santa Fe, Argentina, is a vibrant city located in the central part of the country, serving as the capital of the province of the same name. With a population of approximately 391,164, it plays a crucial role in regional commerce and culture. Its strategic position along the Salado River not only enhances its agricultural significance but also makes it a key transportation hub in South America, attracting visitors and businesses alike.
history: Founded in 1573, Santa Fe has historically served as a critical gateway for trade and cultural exchange in Argentina. Its rich colonial heritage is evident in its architecture and urban layout.
economy: Santa Fe's economy is primarily driven by agriculture, with key industries including grain production, livestock, and dairy farming. The city also hosts various manufacturing and service sectors, contributing to its role as an economic center in the region.
tourism: Visitors to Santa Fe can explore its beautiful parks, historical sites, and vibrant cultural scene. Key attractions include the stunning Cathedral of Santa Fe, the scenic Costanera, and various museums showcasing the city's rich history and art.

best_restaurants.name: La Casa de los Tacos
best_restaurants.description: Offers a unique twist on traditional tacos with local ingredients.

weather_summary: Santa Fe experiences mild winters and hot, humid summers, with occasional rainfall throughout the year.

faqs.question: What time zone is Santa Fe in?
faqs.answer: Santa Fe is in the America/Argentina/Cordoba time zone.

faqs.question: Does Santa Fe observe daylight saving time?
faqs.answer: No, Santa Fe does not observe daylight saving time.

faqs.question: What is the population of Santa Fe?
faqs.answer: The population of Santa Fe is approximately 391,164.

faqs.question: When does sunrise typically occur in Santa Fe?
faqs.answer: Sunrise in Santa Fe typically occurs around 6:30 AM, varying slightly throughout the year.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Santa Fe?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit Santa Fe is during the spring and fall, when temperatures are mild and pleasant.
